chapter: Chapter 8: Measurement of Time and Motion

questions.question: Define speed, velocity, and acceleration with formulas and examples.
questions.answer: Speed = distance/time; Velocity = displacement/time; Acceleration = change in velocity/time. Example: A car travels 100 km in 2 h → speed = 50 km/h; if displacement is 90 km → velocity = 45 km/h; if velocity changes from 0 to 20 m/s in 5 s → acceleration = 4 m/s².

questions.question: A train moves 120 km north in 2 hours and then 80 km east in 2 hours. Calculate average speed and velocity.
questions.answer: Total distance = 120+80=200 km; total time = 4 h; average speed = 200/4=50 km/h. Displacement = √(120²+80²)=√(14400+6400)=√20800 ≈144.22 km; average velocity = 144.22/4 ≈ 36.06 km/h.

questions.question: Derive the equation of motion v = u + at and solve a numerical example.
questions.answer: v = final velocity, u = initial velocity, a = acceleration, t = time. Example: u=0, a=2 m/s², t=5 s → v=0+2×5=10 m/s.

questions.question: A car accelerates from 10 m/s to 30 m/s in 5 s. Find acceleration and distance travelled using equations of motion.
questions.answer: Acceleration: a = (v-u)/t = (30-10)/5 = 4 m/s². Distance: s = ut + ½at² = 10×5 + 0.5×4×25 = 50+50=100 m.
answer_outline: Distance calculation using s = ut + ½at²

questions.question: Explain uniform and non-uniform motion with examples and graphs.
questions.answer: Uniform motion: constant speed (e.g., car moving at 60 km/h). Graph: straight line distance-time graph. Non-uniform motion: varying speed (e.g., accelerating car). Graph: curved distance-time graph.

questions.question: A ball is dropped from a height of 20 m. Calculate the time to reach the ground assuming g=10 m/s².
questions.answer: Using s = ½gt², 20 = 0.5×10×t² → t²=4 → t=2 s.

questions.question: Calculate the distance covered by a car accelerating uniformly from rest at 3 m/s² for 10 s.
A body moves with uniform velocity of 5 m/s. Plot distance-time graph and calculate distance in 12 s.
questions.answer: Distance = speed × time = 5 ×12=60 m. Graph: straight line with slope = 5.

questions.question: Derive s = ut + ½at² from v = u + at and v² = u² + 2as.
questions.answer: From v = u + at → t = (v-u)/a. Substitute in v² = u² + 2as → s = ut + ½at².

questions.question: A car moves 30 km east in 30 min and then 40 km north in 40 min. Calculate average speed and velocity.
questions.answer: Total distance = 30+40=70 km, total time = 70 min = 1.1667 h, average speed = 70/1.1667 ≈ 60 km/h. Displacement = √(30²+40²)=50 km, average velocity=50/1.1667 ≈ 42.86 km/h.

questions.question: Explain the difference between scalar and vector quantities with examples from motion.
questions.answer: Scalar: only magnitude (e.g., speed, distance, time). Vector: magnitude + direction (e.g., velocity, displacement, acceleration). Example: Speed = 50 km/h, Velocity = 50 km/h east.

questions.question: A train accelerates from 20 m/s to 30 m/s in 50 s. Calculate acceleration and distance travelled.
Explain how to measure time intervals using a stopwatch and a pendulum with example calculations.
questions.answer: Stopwatch: direct reading. Pendulum: T=2π√(l/g), e.g., l=1 m → T=2π√(1/9.8) ≈ 2.01 s per swing. Total time = n×T.

questions.question: A particle moves along x-axis with displacement x=5t²+2t. Find velocity and acceleration at t=3 s.
Explain graphical representation of motion: distance-time and velocity-time graphs with examples.
questions.answer: Distance-time: slope = speed, straight line = uniform motion, curved = acceleration. Velocity-time: slope = acceleration, area = displacement.

questions.question: A body starts from rest and accelerates at 2 m/s² for 8 s. Calculate final velocity and distance covered.
Explain the difference between instantaneous speed and average speed with examples.
questions.answer: Instantaneous: speed at a particular instant (speedometer reading). Average: total distance/total time. Example: 100 km in 2 h → average speed = 50 km/h.

questions.question: A cyclist covers 5 km north in 10 min and 3 km south in 5 min. Find average velocity and speed.
questions.answer: Total distance = 5+3=8 km; total time=15 min=0.25 h; average speed=8/0.25=32 km/h. Displacement=5-3=2 km north; average velocity=2/0.25=8 km/h north.

questions.question: Calculate the distance travelled by a particle with velocity v = 4t m/s in 5 s.
A particle moves with acceleration a=3 m/s². If initial velocity u=2 m/s, calculate velocity after 6 s and displacement.
Explain with examples how to measure time using atomic clock and digital stopwatch.
questions.answer: Atomic clock: vibration of atoms → precise time. Digital stopwatch: electronic counting → start/stop intervals. Example: stopwatch measures 12.5 s for reaction time.

questions.question: A ball is thrown vertically upward with 20 m/s. Calculate maximum height and time to reach maximum height. Take g=10 m/s².
questions.answer: v=0 at max height, v=u-at → 0=20-10 t → t=2 s. Max height: s=ut-½gt²=20×2-0.5×10×4=40-20=20 m.

questions.question: Explain motion with uniform acceleration using v²-u²=2as and solve a numerical example.
A particle starts from rest, accelerates at 3 m/s² for 5 s, then moves at constant speed. Calculate total distance travelled in 10 s.
questions.answer: During acceleration: s1=0.5×3×25=37.5 m; velocity at end of acceleration v=3×5=15 m/s; constant speed for 5 s: s2=15×5=75 m. Total distance s=s1+s2=37.5+75=112.5 m.

questions.question: Explain motion of freely falling body using equations of motion and calculate time and velocity for a body falling from 45 m. Take g=10 m/s².
